user_task: End-to-end developer-experience review: install -> setup -> ingest -> query -> cite succeeds on the platform with no broken steps, no stale model names, and a correct, timely answer.
source: JTBD & user tasks tab, DEVELOPER EXPERIENCE (DX) bucket; seed queries from 'NeMo Retriever — JTBD seed queries' tab, DX -> 'walk me through getting set up on a clean machine (install -> load docs -> ask a question -> surface a citation), every stage wired, current model names, no broken steps'.

success_criteria.quality: operational pass — EVERY stage of the happy-path chain succeeds without manual repair: install -> setup -> ingest -> query -> citation surface. No broken steps; no STALE MODEL NAMES (must use the current names: embedder nvidia/llama-nemotron-embed-1b-v2, reranker llama-nemotron-rerank-1b-v2, extraction nemotron-page-elements-v3 -> nemotron-ocr-v2 -> nemotron-table-structure-v1); no 404 on any referenced endpoint. The final query returns a correct, timely answer with a RESOLVABLE (source, page_number) citation. Binary pass/fail.
success_criteria.time: slow — full pipeline (install -> ingest -> query -> cite) <= 10 min on a small corpus; per-stage rungs (1-4) <= 2 min each.
success_criteria.trigger_rate: >= 95% — a 'walk me through the whole pipeline / run end-to-end on my docs' prompt must fire the skill
success_criteria.subcommand_accuracy: >= 90% — must run the happy-path chain `retriever --version` then `retriever ingest <corpus>/` then `retriever query "<q>" --top-k 5`; no --input-type flag (does not exist); model names current

environment.install: Bootstrap per skills/nemo-retriever/references/install.md (auto-detects GPU+CUDA-13 -> '.[local]' cu130 torch; else base install with hosted endpoints). `retriever --version` -> a current dev-stamped version like 2026.06.10.devXXXX (NOT a stale/placeholder version).
environment.distinct_focus: This is a DX 'review' of the WHOLE pipeline wired together — not a single capability and not host-specific like the SETUP suites. The defining assertions: every stage succeeds with no broken step, every referenced model name is CURRENT (no stale names), no referenced endpoint 404s, and the final answer is correct, timely, and carries a resolvable (source, page_number) citation.

current_model_names.note: These are the names the resolved ingest plan / dry-run surfaces (embed_model_name, ocr_version v2, page-elements v3 columns). Any earlier/legacy name (e.g. an unversioned page-elements, ocr-v1-as-default, or a non-llama-nemotron embedder) is a STALE-NAME fail for this DX review.

environment.format_autodetect: `retriever ingest <paths...>` auto-detects PDF from the extension. There is NO --input-type flag.
environment.ingest_success_line: Ingested N file(s) -> M row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/<table>. (branch_summary like pdf:2 shows in --dry-run)
environment.query_hit_shape: JSON array; each hit has exactly {source, page_number, text}; page_number is a 1-indexed int. The citation is the (source, page_number) pair of the grounding hit.
environment.dry_run_grounding: `retriever ingest data/ --dry-run` (offline, no network) confirms branch_summary pdf:N, method pdfium, ocr_version v2, use_page_elements true; pinning --embed-model-name nvidia/llama-nemotron-embed-1b-v2 surfaces embed_model_name + embedding_endpoint in the plan — used here to ground that the chain references CURRENT model names with no 404.

cases.title: Version / install sanity — CLI present, current (non-stale) version, no broken install step
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the DX-review floor: the FIRST stage of the chain (install -> CLI present) succeeds. Rung 1 asserts only that the install completes and `retriever --version` prints a CURRENT dev-stamped version (not a stale/placeholder), so the platform entry point is wired. It does not yet ingest or query. Adds nothing over a prior rung (it is the floor).
cases.prompt: Fresh clone of the platform on a clean machine. Walk me through the install and confirm the retriever CLI is actually there and current — what version do I have?

expected_output.install: install recipe completes with no broken step; prints RETRIEVER_VENV=<abs path>
expected_output.version: `retriever --version` prints a CURRENT dev-stamped version (e.g. 2026.06.10.devXXXX) — NOT a stale placeholder/legacy string
expected_output.no_404: no referenced endpoint or package index 404s during install

pass_when: install per references/install.md completes without a broken step (no manual repair)
`retriever --version` resolves and prints a current dev-stamped version (e.g. 2026.06.10.devXXXX)
version is NOT stale/placeholder; CLI binary is on PATH / at $RETRIEVER_VENV/bin/retriever
no --input-type flag invented (does not exist); no 404 during install

cases.title: Ingest stage succeeds on a small corpus — no broken extraction step, no stale extraction-model names
cases.complexity_axis: Adds the SECOND stage of the chain: `retriever ingest <corpus>/` over a small 2-PDF corpus succeeds with a NON-ZERO row count and no broken extraction step. New dimension over rung 1: the ingest + extraction pipeline is wired and references CURRENT extraction model names (nemotron-page-elements-v3 -> nemotron-ocr-v2 -> nemotron-table-structure-v1; dry-run shows method pdfium, ocr_version v2, use_page_elements true). Catches a broken/stale extraction stage before any query is asked.
cases.prompt: Load these PDFs into the platform from data/ and confirm the ingest stage actually ran end-to-end with no broken step. How many files and rows did it produce?
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER ingest data/ --dry-run   # offline grounding: branch_summary pdf:2, method pdfium, ocr_version v2, use_page_elements true

expected_output.dry_run_plan: branch_summary pdf:2; method pdfium; ocr_version v2; use_page_elements true; extract_text/tables/charts/images all true by default; model names current (no stale page-elements / ocr name)
expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 2 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (N >= 1, NON-ZERO)
expected_output.pipeline: extraction stage fires (nemotron-page-elements-v3 -> nemotron-ocr-v2; table-structure when --use-table-structure); no broken extraction step
expected_output.no_404: no 404 on any referenced extraction endpoint

pass_when: `retriever ingest data/` ingests BOTH PDFs and returns a NON-ZERO row count (no broken extraction step)
format auto-detected as pdf (no --input-type flag); branch_summary pdf:2 in dry-run
extraction stage references CURRENT model names (nemotron-page-elements-v3 / nemotron-ocr-v2) — no stale name
no 404 on any referenced endpoint during ingest

cases.title: Query stage returns a correct, grounded answer — no broken retrieval/embedding step
cases.complexity_axis: Adds the THIRD stage: `retriever query` returns a CORRECT grounded answer (not just hits). New dimension over rung 2: the query/embedding/retrieval stage is wired and references the CURRENT embedder (nvidia/llama-nemotron-embed-1b-v2); the answer is grounded in the corpus content. Still single-doc; does not yet assert the citation resolves to (source, page). Catches a broken query stage or a stale embedder name.
cases.prompt: I loaded data/woods_frost.pdf. Now ask the platform a question and make sure the query stage works: in which poem does the speaker say they have 'miles to go before I sleep'?
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "miles to go before I sleep" --top-k 5

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever.
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array of hits; each {source, page_number, text}; >= 1 hit citing woods_frost.pdf p1
expected_output.answer: 'Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening' by Robert Frost (the line 'miles to go before I sleep' is on p1); grounded in woods_frost.pdf p1
expected_output.embedder: query/embedding stage references the CURRENT embedder nvidia/llama-nemotron-embed-1b-v2 — no stale name

pass_when: query stage runs and returns >= 1 grounded hit citing woods_frost.pdf
answer is correct: 'Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening' (poem containing 'miles to go before I sleep')
no broken query/embedding step; embedder name current (nvidia/llama-nemotron-embed-1b-v2)
no 404 on the referenced embedding/retrieval endpoint

cases.title: Citation surfaces and RESOLVES — (source, page_number) points to the right page
cases.complexity_axis: Adds the FOURTH stage: the citation must SURFACE and RESOLVE. New dimension over rung 3: the answer must come from a table cell whose grounding hit carries a non-null (source, page_number) that actually resolves to the correct page of the correct file. Forces the table-aware extraction path (--use-table-structure -> nemotron-table-structure-v1) and a content-typed query. Catches a citation that is missing, null, or points to the wrong source/page.
cases.prompt: From data/table_test.pdf, what value did James have in 2019 — and give me the source pointer (file + page) so I can verify it. No skipped citation.

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (table rows present)
expected_output.pipeline: --use-table-structure -> use_table_structure true; nemotron-table-structure-v1 fires after page-elements v3 + ocr v2 (all current names)
expected_output.citation: grounding hit carries (source=table_test.pdf, page_number=1) — a resolvable pointer to the cell's page; page_number is a 1-indexed int

pass_when: answer is exactly 978
the answer's grounding hit carries a NON-NULL (source, page_number) citation that RESOLVES to table_test.pdf p1
table cell recovered via --use-table-structure (nemotron-table-structure-v1) + --content-types text,table — no silent text-only fallback that loses the cell
citation surfaces (not skipped); points to the correct source and page; no 404

cases.title: Acceptance: FULL chain install->ingest->query->cite, no broken step, no stale name, correct timely cited answer <= 10 min
cases.complexity_axis: Composed operational-pass / acceptance gate for the DX-review row, and the DISTINCT focus of this suite: the WHOLE pipeline integrated, not one capability. Adds over rung 4: all four stages run in ONE end-to-end pass on a small 3-PDF corpus, asserting simultaneously that (a) every stage succeeds with no broken step, (b) every referenced model name is CURRENT (no stale), (c) no referenced endpoint 404s, (d) the final answer is correct and carries a resolvable (source, page_number) citation, and (e) the whole thing finishes <= 10 min. This is the gate the other rungs build up to.
cases.prompt: Do a full end-to-end review on a clean machine: install the platform, load the whole data/ folder, then ask a question and answer it WITH a source pointer — in which poem does the speaker say they have 'miles to go before I sleep'? Confirm every stage (install, ingest, query, citation) ran with no broken step, the model names are current, nothing 404'd, and the whole pipeline finished in under 10 minutes.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "miles to go before I sleep" --top-k 5

expected_output.version: current dev-stamped version (e.g. 2026.06.10.devXXXX); no stale string
expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 3 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (branch_summary pdf:3; N NON-ZERO)
expected_output.answer: 'Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening' by Robert Frost
expected_output.citation: grounding hit carries (source=woods_frost.pdf, page_number=1) — resolves to the page containing 'miles to go before I sleep'
assertions: every stage succeeds with NO broken step (install -> version -> ingest -> query -> citation), no manual repair

all referenced model names CURRENT: embedder nvidia/llama-nemotron-embed-1b-v2, reranker llama-nemotron-rerank-1b-v2, extraction nemotron-page-elements-v3 -> nemotron-ocr-v2 -> nemotron-table-structure-v1 — no stale names
final answer correct + timely, with a resolvable (source, page_number) citation
wall clock (clean state -> cited answer) <= 10 min on the small corpus

pass_when: full chain install -> `--version` -> `ingest data/` -> `query` runs end-to-end with NO broken step (no manual repair)
ingest produces NON-ZERO rows over all 3 PDFs (branch_summary pdf:3)
final answer correct: 'Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening'
answer carries a resolvable (source, page_number) citation: woods_frost.pdf p1
NO stale model names anywhere in the chain; NO 404 on any referenced endpoint
end-to-end wall clock <= 10 min
